sourceMovie.title: Sky High
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Sky High (2005) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Big Hero 6
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sky High clicked for you, Big Hero 6 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sky High clicked for you, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Incredibles
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sky High clicked for you, The Incredibles (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sky High?
faq.answer: Try Big Hero 6, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa, Secret Headquarters—each shares DNA with Sky High while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sky High?
faq.answer: Sky High (2005) is best described as Adventure, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sky High worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
